Imagine being fifteen years old and thinking you are too small to make a difference. Imagine sitting alone outside a government building with a hand-painted sign. Imagine your parents trying to stop you. Your classmates refusing to join you. Strangers looking at you with pity. But you keep going anyway. This is the story of three girls who proved that one small person can change the world. They showed that your voice matters, even as a child. They showed that you do not have to wait to be great.
